title: 10 best herbal essential oils for amenorrhea
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for amenorrhea are natural remedies derived from plants that help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate the symptoms of amenorrhea, a condition characterized by the absence of menstruation. The best herbal essential oils for amenorrhea include Angelica sinensis, Paeonia lactiflora, Vitex agnus-castus, Cimicifuga racemosa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, Avena sativa, Tribulus terrestris, and Angelica archangelica. If left untreated, amenorrhea can lead to emotional distress, infertility issues, and a range of physical symptoms that significantly impact daily life, affecting relationships, mental health, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica essential oils helps with amenorrhea because of its unique ability to balance hormonal levels in the body. The oil contains angelic acid, a compound that has been shown to stimulate the production of progesterone, a hormone crucial for ovulation and menstruation. Additionally, angelica essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pelvic pain and cramping associated with amenorrhea. By regulating hormonal imbalances and alleviating discomfort, herbal angelica essential oils can promote a healthy menstrual cycle and alleviate the symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Stimulates uterine contractions: Angelica essential oil helps to stimulate uterine contractions, which can aid in menstruation and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
Regulates hormone balance: The essential oil has been shown to regulate hormonal imbalances, which is often a root cause of amenorrhea, helping to promote regular menstrual cycles.
Relieves cramps and discomfort: Angelica essential oil's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties can help alleviate menstrual cramps, pain, and discomfort associated with amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Angelica roots have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in reducing menstrual cramps and regulating hormonal imbalances associated with amenorrhea.
Seeds: Angelica seeds contain phytoestrogens that help regulate estrogen levels, promoting a normal menstrual cycle and alleviating symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica leaves and stems in the early morning when they are most aromatic.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12-18 hours or air dry for several days.
Steam distill the dried angelica archangelica with distilled water at a ratio of 1:10 for 4-6 hours to extract essential oils.
Filter the distillation mixture through a coffee filter paper or cheesecloth to separate the oil from the water.
Store the resulting essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and keep it refrigerated until use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with amenorrhea because of its unique ability to regulate hormonal imbalances, which is often at the root of this condition. The oil's potent compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, have been shown to normalize estrogen levels, reducing symptoms like irregular periods and heavy bleeding. Additionally, sage essential oils' natural anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the uterus, promoting a healthy environment for menstruation to occur. By using sage essential oils, women with amenorrhea may experience relief from this frustrating and often debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: This property helps to relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to irregular or absent menstrual periods.

Uterine stimulant property: Sage essential oil's ability to stimulate the uterus can help regulate menstrual cycles and increase blood flow to the pelvic region, which may help restore regular menstruation in cases of amenorrhea.

Astringent property: The astringent properties of sage essential oil may help to reduce excessive bleeding or clotting associated with irregular periods, promoting a balanced hormonal environment that can lead to normal menstrual function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving menstrual cramps and regulating hormonal imbalances associated with amenorrhea.
Barks: Sage barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation, promoting regular menstruation in individuals with amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh salvia sclarea leaves and flowers at peak blooming.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, dry place for 7-10 days.
Steam distill 50 grams of dried salvia sclarea material in a still using 500 ml water for 3 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the distillation residue.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and preserve its quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium essential oils helps with amenorrhea because it has a balancing effect on the body's hormonal system, particularly in regulating estrogen levels. The oil's natural properties can stimulate uterine contractions, promoting menstrual regularity and flow. Additionally, geranium oil's ability to reduce anxiety and stress can help alleviate emotional factors that may contribute to amenorrhea. By addressing these underlying issues, geranium essential oils can promote a healthy menstrual cycle and support overall reproductive well-being.
remedy_properties: Uterine Tonic: Geranium oil helps to tone the uterus, which can aid in regularizing menstrual cycles and alleviating amenorrhea caused by uterine problems or hormonal imbalances.

Hormone Regulator: The oil's ability to regulate hormones can help balance estrogen and progesterone levels, which is essential for menstruation to occur normally and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.

Stress Relief: Geranium oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may be contributing to amenorrhea by regulating the body's hormonal response to stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of phytoestrogens, which help regulate hormone imbalances that can lead to amenorrhea.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves and stems, dried for 2 hours in the shade to preserve their properties.
Combine 20 grams of dried pelargonium graveolens with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Steam distill the mixture using a copper alembic still for 4 hours, collecting the essential oils and separating them from the carrier oil.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities or debris, yielding approximately 2 milliliters of pure pelargonium graveolens essential oil.
Transfer the filtered essential oil to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool place for at least 24 hours before use to allow the fragrance to mature and stabilize.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with amenorrhea because it has a calming effect on the body, particularly on the uterus. Its estrogenic properties can help regulate menstrual cycles and stimulate uterine contractions, which can alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea. Additionally, lavender's ability to reduce stress and anxiety can also contribute to its benefits in treating amenorrhea by promoting a healthy hormonal balance, which is essential for normal menstruation. Regular use of English lavender essential oils may help restore menstrual cycles and promote overall reproductive health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: English lavender essential oil has antispasmodic properties, which can help to relieve cramps and spasms associated with menstrual irregularities, thereby potentially promoting regular menstruation.

Adaptogenic: Lavender's adaptogenic properties enable it to balance the body's response to stress, which is often a contributing factor to amenorrhea; by reducing stress levels, lavender may help regulate hormonal imbalances and promote menstrual regularity.

Hormone-regulating: Some research suggests that English lavender essential oil may have a direct impact on hormone regulation, particularly with regards to estrogen, which can help address underlying hormonal imbalances associated with amenorrhea and support the return of menstruation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are used to create essential oils that help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of dysmenorrhea.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a unique combination of fatty acids and flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve uterine contractions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers and dry them in shade for 7-10 days or air-dry for 2 weeks.
Crush dried flowers into fine powder using mortar and pestle, approximately 250 grams per batch.
Mix 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka with 250 grams of powdered lavandula angustifolia and store in glass jar for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discard solids and reserve liquid.
Distill the infused liquid using steam distillation apparatus at atmospheric pressure for approximately 2 hours to produce essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with amenorrhea because of its natural ability to soothe and regulate hormonal imbalances in the body. The calming properties of chamomile help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of irregular menstrual cycles. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps, which can contribute to amenorrhea.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of chamomile oil help to relax the uterine muscles and alleviate spasms, which can also contribute to menstrual cramps and irregular periods (amenorrhea).

Hormone-regulating property: Chamomile oil has been shown to have hormone-balancing effects, which may help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea by promoting a healthy balance of estrogen and progesterone levels.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain high amounts of apigenin, a flavonoid that helps regulate hormonal balance and reduce inflammation, making them ideal for treating amenorrhea.
Roots: Chamomile roots have been traditionally used to stimulate menstrual flow and alleviate cramps, making them a popular choice for essential oils used in the treatment of amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of matricaria chamomilla in quantities of 250 grams.
Steam distill the dried flowers for 3 hours using 500 milliliters of water.
Filter the resulting oil mixture through a coffee filter paper into dark glass bottles.
Store the extracted essential oils at room temperature away from direct sunlight.
Use the extracted essential oils within 6 months of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow essential oils helps with amenorrhea because of its ability to stimulate blood flow and promote uterine health. The oil's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to balance hormones and regulate menstrual cycles. Additionally, yarrow essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the uterus, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with amenorrhea. By promoting a healthy uterine environment, yarrow essential oil can help to restore normal menstruation patterns and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Emmenagogue property: Yarrow essential oil has been shown to stimulate menstrual flow and regulate menstrual cycles, making it an effective natural remedy for amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances or uterine fibroids.

Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties: Yarrow essential oil's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties help to reduce pelvic pain and cramps associated with dysmenorrhea, a common cause of amenorrhea, thereby promoting regular menstrual cycles.

Tonic and uterotonic properties: Yarrow essential oil's tonic and uterotonic properties stimulate the uterus and promote healthy uterine contractions, which can help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances or uterine issues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in sufficient quantities for the desired yield of essential oil.
Steam distill the harvested plant material using a suitable apparatus such as a clevenger or a rotary evaporator at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect the obtained essential oil by condensing the steam vapors and filtering it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Combine the filtered essential oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a 5% concentration ratio for better absorption and stability.
Store the prepared herbal essential oil blend in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight and heat sources at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with amenorrhea because it has a profound impact on the hormonal balance in the body. The oil's natural antispasmodic properties help to relax and calm the uterine muscles, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with amenorrhea. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanly rinse the harvested plant material with distilled water to remove impurities.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 4-6 hours or air dry it naturally.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a still with a 1:2 ratio of plant material to steam.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil using a coffee filter or cheesecloth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion essential oils helps with amenorrhea because of its ability to stimulate the uterus and improve blood flow. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated, ensuring optimal potency.
Wash the collected plant material gently with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities, then pat dry thoroughly.
Steam distill the cleaned taraxacum officinale material using a clevenger apparatus at atmospheric pressure for approximately 4-6 hours.
Collect the obtained essential oil in a clean glass container, filtering it through a coffee filter paper to remove any particulate matter, and storing it in an amber glass bottle.
Allow the taraxacum officinale essential oil to mature for a minimum of 24 hours before use, allowing the fragrance and properties to stabilize fully.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover essential oils helps with amenorrhea because it has been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalances. This can help to stimulate uterine contractions, promote blood flow, and restore normal menstrual function. remedy_properties: Phytoestrogens: Red clover contains isoflavones, particularly formononetin and biochanin A, which have estrogenic effects and may help alleviate symptoms associated with hormone imbalances.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Red clover extracts contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in red clover may help protect the body from free radical damage, which can contribute to hormonal imbalance and other health issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of red clover have been traditionally used to stimulate menstrual flow and alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh trifolium pratense flowers at dawn in mid-summer when they are fully bloomed and dry them immediately.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a clevenger apparatus or similar equipment for 3-4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a separatory funnel and store it in dark glass vials at 10°c.
Mix the essential oil with carrier oils such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a ratio of 1:2 to create a topical blend.
Allow the blend to mature for 24 hours before use and store it in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with amenorrhea because of its ability to stimulate the reproductive system and promote hormonal balance. By applying valerian essential oil topically or inhaling it through aromatherapy, individuals with amenorrhea may experience an increase in estrogen levels, which can help to restore normal menstruation patterns. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots at dawn when the dew is still present for optimal oil yield.
Wash the harvested roots thoroughly in cold running water and gently scrub away any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for efficient solvent extraction.
Steam-distill 200 grams of chopped valeriana officinalis root in 1000 milliliters of distilled water at a temperature of 100°c for three hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation residue using a decantation method and store it in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat amenorrhea with herbal essential oils is to combine Clary Sage, Geranium, and Lavender. Clary Sage helps regulate hormones, while Geranium balances female reproductive organs. Lavender calms the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate amenorrhea. Blend 5-7 drops of each oil with a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba and apply topically to the lower abdomen three times daily. Additionally, inhale the blend through steam inhalation or use in a warm bath for enhanced benefits.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to amenorrhea that are treated with herbal essential oils are dysmenorrhea, polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), endometriosis, and menstrual cramps. These conditions can benefit from the use of herbal essential oils such as clary sage, lavender, and geranium, which have natural estrogen-balancing and anti-inflammatory properties. These oils can be used in aromatherapy or topically applied to help alleviate symptoms like pain, irregular bleeding, and mood swings associated with these conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis essential oils helps with amenorrhea because it has been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles and stimulate uterine contractions. The oil contains compounds such as ferulic acid and ligustilide, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and estrogenic properties. These properties may help to restore normal menstrual function by reducing inflammation and promoting hormonal balance in the body, thereby addressing the underlying causes of amenorrhea and promoting regular menstrual cycles. It may also help to ease menstrual cramps and discomfort associated with the condition.
remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: This sesquiterpene lactone helps with amenorrhea by promoting uterine contractions and regulating menstrual cycles.
Butylphthalide: This sesquiterpene lactone helps with amenorrhea by improving blood circulation to the uterus and promoting menstrual regularity.
Acetophenone: This phenolic compound helps with amenorrhea by reducing inflammation and promoting hormonal balance in the body, which can help regulate menstrual cycles.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Angelica sinensis are used to make essential oils for amenorrhea due to their rich content of ferulic acid, which helps regulate menstrual cycles.
Leaves: The leaves of Angelica sinensis are used to make essential oils for amenorrhea due to their high concentration of ligustilide, a compound that helps stimulate uterine contractions.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Angelica sinensis are used to make essential oils for amenorrhea due to their content of butylphthalide, a compound that helps to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce menstrual pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh angelica sinensis roots in the early morning or late evening when the plant is at its peak potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried roots in a 1:5 ratio with water at 100°c for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separator or decanter and filter it through a coffee filter.
Store the extracted angelica sinensis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora essential oils helps with amenorrhea because it has been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles and promote hormonal balance. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These constituents help with amenorrhea by regulating hormonal balance and improving blood circulation to the uterus, thereby promoting menstrual regularity.
Isoflavones: Isoflavones in Paeonia lactiflora help with amenorrhea by exhibiting estrogen-like properties, which can help to balance hormonal imbalances and stimulate menstrual cycles.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh paeonia lactiflora flowers when they are in full bloom and the petals are fully opened.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus essential oils helps with amenorrhea because they stimulate the pituitary gland to regulate hormonal imbalances that often lead to irregular menstrual cycles. By regulating the body's hormonal flow, Vitex agnus-castus essential oils can help induce menstruation and alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea. remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help regulate menstrual cycles and improve fertility by influencing hormonal balance and ovulation.
Diterpenes: Diterpenes in Vitex agnus-castus essential oils have been found to have a positive effect on the reproductive system, particularly in regulating menstrual cycles and improving fertility.

remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Vitex agnus-castus are primarily used to make essential oils for amenorrhea because they contain the highest concentration of iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for the plant's hormonal balancing properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh vitex agnus-castus berries during the blooming season for optimal potency.
Dry the berries in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Steam distill the dried berries using a 1:4 ratio with water for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water through decantation or centrifugation for 1 hour.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and achieve a clear consistency within 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa essential oils helps with amenorrhea because it has a long history of use in traditional medicine to regulate menstrual cycles. Furanoergosterol: This compound may have a modulating effect on the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, helping to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Cimicifuga racemosa are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds like actein and cimiracemoside, which are believed to help regulate menstrual cycles.

remedy_recipe: Harvest cimicifuga racemosa roots in late fall or early spring when the plant is dormant for maximum potency.

Buds: Employed in essential oil production due to their potential to stimulate blood flow and hormone balance, which can aid in treating amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 kg of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late summer or early fall.
Dry the leaves in a well-ventilated area at 30°c for 5-7 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill 5 kg of dried leaves in a 50-liter copper still with 10 kg of water for 2 hours.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distillate using a condenser and a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron membrane and store it in dark glass bottles at 5°c for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with amenorrhea because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties are thought to help regulate menstrual cycles by reducing inflammation in the pelvic area and promoting hormonal balance. Additionally, the oil's ability to stimulate digestion and improve circulation may also contribute to its amenorrheic benefits. By supporting overall reproductive health, Zingiber officinale essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea, promoting a return to regular menstrual cycles. Its natural properties make it a promising natural remedy.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpenoid compounds help with amenorrhea by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate hormonal imbalances.
Shogaols: These compounds, similar to gingerols, have been found to have anti-inflammatory effects and can help restore menstrual cycles by reducing oxidative stress and promoting hormonal balance.

Volatile Phenolic Compounds (e.g., Zingiberene): These compounds have been shown to have estrogenic activity, which can help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea symptoms by stimulating uterine contractions and promoting estrogenic effects.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These are the underground stems of the plant, which are commonly used due to their high concentration of active compounds that help regulate menstrual cycles.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to produce essential oils, as they contain compounds that help stimulate uterine contractions and promote menstrual flow.

Leaves: While less commonly used than the rhyzomes and roots, the leaves of the plant are sometimes used to produce essential oils that help regulate hormonal imbalances and promote menstrual regularity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal oil yield.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a warm place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content to 12%.
Steam distill the dried rhizomes using a distillation apparatus at a temperature of 100-150°c for 3-4 hours.
Collect the resulting distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separation funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter and store it in a dark glass bottle for up to 1 year.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oils helps with amenorrhea because it is a natural remedy that supports hormonal balance and menstrual health. The oil contains apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to stimulate the pituitary gland and promote the release of gonadotropin-releasing hormone, which regulates menstrual cycles. Additionally, Avena sativa has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to soothe and calm the uterine lining, promoting a regular menstrual cycle and reducing symptoms of amenorrhea. This natural approach may help to restore menstrual regularity and promote overall reproductive health.
remedy_constituents: Lignans: Help with amenorrhea by regulating the hormonal balance, particularly estrogen levels, which play a crucial role in menstrual cycles.
Furofuran lignans: Aid in the treatment of amenorrhea by exerting antioxidant effects and modulating the body's response to stress, which can disrupt menstrual cycles.

Terpenoids: Contribute to alleviating amenorrhea symptoms by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties and regulating the endocrine system's functioning, which is essential for maintaining regular menstrual cycles.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Avena sativa flowers are used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpenes and other compounds that help regulate the menstrual cycle.
Seeds: Avena sativa seeds are used for their high content of fatty acids and phytosterols, which can help stimulate menstrual flow and balance hormonal imbalances.
Leaves: Avena sativa leaves are used for their antioxidant properties and ability to regulate hormonal imbalances, promoting menstrual regularity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried avena sativa in late summer when the plant is mature and the seeds are fully formed.
Steam distill the dried plant material at 100-110 degrees celsius for 3-4 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus using a separating funnel and a glass container.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity.
Store the final herbal avena sativa essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Tribulus terrestris essential oils helps with amenorrhea because it promotes hormone balance and stimulates ovulation, addressing the underlying causes of irregular periods. The oil's phytoestrogens mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, supporting the reproductive system and menstrual cycle. Additionally, its diuretic properties help to reduce water retention and alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea, such as bloating and discomfort. By regulating hormonal imbalances, Tribulus terrestris essential oils support the body's natural ability to regulate menstrual cycles.
remedy_constituents: Furostanol Saponins: Help regulate menstrual cycles by promoting the release of gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH), which in turn stimulates the release of luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH), essential for ovulation and menstrual cycle maintenance.
Tribuloside: Stimulate the release of GnRH, LH, and FSH, which helps regulate ovulation and menstrual cycle, addressing the root cause of amenorrhea.
Terrestrin: May help regulate the hormonal balance by influencing the activity of the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, which plays a crucial role in menstrual cycle regulation and ovulation.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are rich in saponins, which are believed to have a stimulating effect on the uterus and help regulate menstrual cycles.
Roots: They contain bioactive compounds that may help to balance hormonal imbalances and promote menstrual regularity.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh tribulus terrestris flowers and leaves at 8 am on a dry sunny morning.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100°c for 3 hours with a 1:5 ratio.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle at 4°c for 24 hours before use.
Perform a quality control test on the final product to ensure its purity and potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica essential oils helps with amenorrhea because of its hormonal balancing properties. It is believed to stimulate the pituitary gland, which regulates menstrual cycles, and promote the production of follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) and luteinizing hormone (LH). These hormones play a crucial role in ovulation and menstrual flow. Additionally, Angelica archangelica has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and promote a healthy reproductive system, thereby supporting the return of menstrual cycles in individuals with amenorrhea.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and hormone-balancing properties, which may help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea symptoms.
Phenolic glycosides: These compounds have estrogen-like properties, which may help stimulate uterine contractions, promote menstrual flow, and alleviate amenorrhea.

Angelica lactone: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and promote hormonal balance, both of which may contribute to the regulation of menstrual cycles and the alleviation of amenorrhea symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Angelica archangelica are used because they contain bioactive compounds that may help to stimulate menstrual flow and alleviate menstrual disorders.

Leaves: The leaves of Angelica archangelica are used because they contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory and estrogenic properties that may help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Angelica archangelica are used because they contain bioactive compounds that may help to stimulate uterine contractions and promote menstrual flow, addressing amenorrhea symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 15-20 angelica archangelica roots in the early morning and clean them with water to remove impurities.
Dry the roots in a well-ventilated area at 35-40°c for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried angelica archangelica roots in a ratio of 1:3 with water for 2 hours to extract essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the extracted angelica archangelica essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid to preserve quality.
